wav_writer.c(3) Library Functions Manual wav_writer.c(3)

NAME

wav_writer.c - Simple WAV file writer implementation for audio debugging.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct wav_riff_header_t
WAV RIFF header (first 12 bytes of WAV file). struct wav_fmt_chunk_t
WAV format chunk describing audio format parameters. struct wav_data_header_t
WAV data chunk header preceding audio samples.

Functions


wav_writer_t * wav_writer_open (const char *filepath, int sample_rate, int channels)
int wav_writer_write (wav_writer_t *writer, const float *samples, int num_samples)
void wav_writer_close (wav_writer_t *writer)
bool wav_dump_enabled (void)

Detailed Description

Simple WAV file writer implementation for audio debugging.

Definition in file wav_writer.c.

Function Documentation

bool wav_dump_enabled (void )

Definition at line 138 of file wav_writer.c.

138                             {
139   const char *env = SAFE_GETENV("ASCIICHAT_DUMP_AUDIO");
140   return env && (strcmp(env, "1") == 0 || strcmp(env, "true") == 0);
141 }




Referenced by audio_analysis_init(), and
    audio_client_init().






void
  wav_writer_close (wav_writer_t * writer)


Definition at line 112 of file wav_writer.c.


112                                             {
113   if (!writer) {
114     return;
115   }
116 
117   if (writer->file) {
118     // Update file size and data size in header
119     uint32_t data_size = (uint32_t)(writer->samples_written * sizeof(float));
120     uint32_t file_size = data_size + 36; // 36 = header size without RIFF chunk
121 
122     // Seek to file size field (offset 4)
123     fseek(writer->file, 4, SEEK_SET);
124     fwrite(&file_size, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, writer->file);
125 
126     // Seek to data size field (offset 40)
127     fseek(writer->file, 40, SEEK_SET);
128     fwrite(&data_size, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, writer->file);
129 
130     // Flush all buffered data to ensure headers are written before closing
131     fflush(writer->file);
132     fclose(writer->file);
133   }
134 
135   SAFE_FREE(writer);
136 }






Referenced by audio_analysis_destroy(),
    audio_cleanup(), audio_client_init(), and
    client_audio_pipeline_destroy().






wav_writer_t
  * wav_writer_open (const char * filepath, int sample_rate, int
  channels)


Definition at line 48 of file wav_writer.c.


48                                                                                    {
49   if (!filepath || sample_rate <= 0 || channels <= 0 || channels > 2) {
50     return NULL;
51   }
52 
53   wav_writer_t *writer = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(wav_writer_t), wav_writer_t *);
54   if (!writer) {
55     return NULL;
56   }
57 
58   writer->file = platform_fopen("file_stream", filepath, "wb");
59   if (!writer->file) {
60     SAFE_FREE(writer);
61     return NULL;
62   }
63 
64   writer->samples_written = 0;
65   writer->sample_rate = sample_rate;
66   writer->channels = channels;
67 
68   // Write WAV header (we'll update sizes when closing)
69   wav_riff_header_t riff = {.riff = {'R', 'I', 'F', 'F'},
70                             .file_size = 0, // Will update on close
71                             .wave = {'W', 'A', 'V', 'E'}};
72 
73   wav_fmt_chunk_t fmt = {.fmt = {'f', 'm', 't', ' '},
74                          .chunk_size = 16,
75                          .audio_format = 3, // IEEE float
76                          .num_channels = (uint16_t)channels,
77                          .sample_rate = (uint32_t)sample_rate,
78                          .byte_rate = (uint32_t)(sample_rate * channels * sizeof(float)),
79                          .block_align = (uint16_t)(channels * sizeof(float)),
80                          .bits_per_sample = 32};
81 
82   wav_data_header_t data = {
83       .data = {'d', 'a', 't', 'a'},
84       .data_size = 0 // Will update on close
85   };
86 
87   fwrite(&riff, sizeof(riff), 1, writer->file);
88   fwrite(&fmt, sizeof(fmt), 1, writer->file);
89   fwrite(&data, sizeof(data), 1, writer->file);
90 
91   return writer;
92 }






References platform_fopen().


Referenced by audio_analysis_init(),
    audio_client_init(), and client_audio_pipeline_create().






int
  wav_writer_write (wav_writer_t * writer, const float * samples, int
  num_samples)


Definition at line 94 of file wav_writer.c.


94                                                                                   {
95   if (!writer || !writer->file || !samples || num_samples <= 0) {
96     return -1;
97   }
98 
99   size_t written = fwrite(samples, sizeof(float), num_samples, writer->file);
100   if (written != (size_t)num_samples) {
101     return -1;
102   }
103 
104   writer->samples_written += num_samples;
105 
106   // Flush immediately to ensure data is written to disk (critical for real-time analysis)
107   fflush(writer->file);
108 
109   return 0;
110 }






Referenced by audio_analysis_track_received_sample(),
    audio_analysis_track_sent_sample(),
    audio_process_received_samples(), and
    client_audio_pipeline_process_duplex().
